news.summary: APi Group Corporation (NYSE: APG) disclosed that its senior leadership will engage in a fireside chat at the William Blair 45th Annual Growth Stock Conference on June 5th at 11:20 a.m. CT. The webcast link for the event and a replay will be accessible on APi's Investor Relations webpage at www.apigroupinc.com. Those interested are encouraged to visit the company's website for additional details.
news.headline: APi Group to participate in conferences on June 5th, including William Blair 45th Annual Growth Stock Conference. Livestream available at www.apigroupinc.com.

news.summary: Distribution Solutions Group, Inc. (NASDAQ: DSGR), a leading specialty distribution company based in Fort Worth, Texas, revealed its participation in the upcoming 15th Annual East Coast IDEAS Conference at the Westin Times Square, New York City, on June 12, 2025. The company's management will be engaging in investor meetings and is scheduled to present from 8:35 a.m. to 9:10 a.m. ET on the same day. The presentation will be webcast and open to all interested parties.
news.headline: Distribution Solutions Group, Inc. (NASDAQ: DSGR) to host 1x1 meetings at the 15th Annual East Coast IDEAS Conference on June 12, 2025 in NYC.

news.summary: Cutting-edge infrastructure combining artificial intelligence (AI), encryption, and blockchain technology is paving the way for the future of data control and revenue generation for institutions. This advanced infrastructure is designed to ensure data sovereignty and facilitate institutional monetization. By integrating AI, encryption, and blockchain, this next-generation system is revolutionizing how data is managed and secured. This innovative approach is poised to shape the future of data governance, with institutions able to capitalize on the power of their data in a more secure and efficient manner.
news.headline: VCI Global introducing 'Project QG' - AI, encryption, and blockchain platform reshaping global infrastructure.

news.summary: In New York and Armonk, N.Y., JDC Power Systems, an electrical systems integrator catering specifically to the data center industry, has named John Raio as its CEO. With over 25 years of leadership in the data center and critical infrastructure fields, Raio assumes the role from his previous position at Quality Uptime Services, a prominent independent critical power service provider in America. The appointment comes into effect immediately, showcasing a strategic move by JDC to leverage Raio's extensive experience in the sector.
news.headline: JDC Power Systems appoints John Raio as CEO, with over 25 years experience in data center industry.
Gold exploration at Mine Centre project in Ontario will target past producing Foley Shaft as part of Q-Gold's strategic program.

news.summary: A simplified study design focusing on the United States continues the progress of Phase 2 trials for IHL-42X in treating obstructive sleep apnea. This streamlined approach aims to capitalize on the momentum achieved during the previous phases of the study. The emphasis on the U.S. market suggests a strategic shift and underscores the potential significance of the findings. This approach could enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of the research process, potentially leading to quicker and more meaningful results within this population.
news.headline: Incannex Healthcare Inc. moves IHL-42X to Phase 3 trial after FDA approval, focusing on obstructive sleep apnea in the U.S.

news.summary: On May 29, 2025, Bombardier revealed that the Global 7500 and Challenger 3500 aircraft will be showcased at the Catarina Aviation Show in São Paulo, Brazil, from June 5-7. This display aims to highlight the outstanding features of these aircraft models for potential clients in the region. Along with the aircraft showcase, Bombardier's sales team will be present at the event to engage with customers.
news.headline: Bombardier to exhibit Global 7500 and Challenger 3500 jets at Catarina Aviation Show in Brazil from June 5-7. Sales team to meet customers.

news.summary: Today, Canadian Life Companies Split Corp. revealed their plan to initiate a Normal Course Issuer Bid (NCIB) for the purchase of their Preferred Shares and Class A Shares. The Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX) has approved their notice of intention for this bid. Starting on June 2, 2025, the NCIB will run through June 1, 2026. Shares will be acquired through the TSX and/or other Canadian trading systems during this period.
news.headline: Canadian Life Companies Split Corp. approved to buy back Preferred and Class A Shares on TSX starting June 2, 2025, till June 1, 2026.

news.summary: On May 29, 2025, Quantum BioPharma Ltd. (NASDAQ: QNTM) (CSE: QNTM) (FRA: 0K91) disclosed that it has enlisted audit firm MNP LLP to perform PCAOB audits of the Company’s financial statements. This move is in preparation for a potential initial public offering. The company behind unbuzzd TM, Unbuzzd Wellness Inc. (formerly Celly Nutrition Corp.), introduced a beverage proven to hasten alcohol metabolism, enhance mental clarity, and alleviate hangover symptoms. The previous auditor, Stern & Lovrics LLP, resigned upon the Company’s request, leading to the appointment of MNP. The new audit firm will serve until the conclusion of the subsequent annual meeting of the Company’s shareholders after starting on May 20, 2025.
news.headline: Quantum BioPharma licensee Celly Nutrition renamed Unbuzzd Wellness Inc., hires MNP LLP for IPO audits. Former auditor Stern & Lovrics LLP resigned effective May 20, 2025.

news.summary: A recent TM Forum Catalyst project showcased the transformation of APIs by telcos, highlighting their evolution into scalable, high-quality on-demand services. This innovation aims to create lucrative revenue opportunities for telecommunications companies.
news.headline: Optiva, PlektonLabs & Qeema showcase telco transformation at DTW Ignite event with API evolution for high-quality, scalable services.

news.summary: Today, Canadian Banc Corp. revealed its plan to conduct a Normal Course Issuer Bid (NCIB) on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX) by purchasing its Preferred Shares and Class A Shares. The NCIB, approved by the TSX, is scheduled to initiate on June 2, 2025, and conclude on June 1, 2026. This buyback program signifies the company's intention to repurchase its own shares within this timeframe using TSX facilities or other Canadian trading platforms.
news.headline: Canadian Banc Corp. to buy Preferred and Class A Shares, TSX acceptance for NCIB starting June 2, 2025; ends June 1, 2026.

news.summary: In Toronto on May 29, 2025, Dividend 15 Split Corp. II revealed it had received approval from the Toronto Stock Exchange to proceed with a Normal Course Issuer Bid (NCIB). This initiative allows the Company to buy back its Preferred Shares and Class A Shares via the TSX or alternate Canadian trading systems. The NCIB is scheduled to start on June 2, 2025, and will conclude on June 1, 2026.
news.headline: Dividend 15 Split Corp. II allowed to buy back shares on TSX; NCIB to start on June 2, 2025, ending June 1, 2026.

news.summary: On May 29, 2025, North American Financial 15 Split Corp. revealed its plans for a Normal Course Issuer Bid (NCIB) to buy back its Preferred Shares and Class A Shares through the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX) and other Canadian trading platforms. The TSX has approved the Company's proposal, with the NCIB set to start on June 2, 2025, and finalize on June 1, 2026.
news.headline: North American Financial 15 Split Corp. is launching a Normal Course Issuer Bid on TSX from June 2, 2025 to June 1, 2026.

news.summary: RISHON LE ZION, Israel - On May 29, 2025, BOS Better Online Solutions Ltd. (NASDAQ: BOSC) announced its financial performance for the first quarter of 2025.
news.headline: BOS reports record $15 million revenues in Q1 2025 - RISHON LE ZION, Israel (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) - BOS Better Online Solutions Ltd.

news.summary: On May 29, 2025, Financial 15 Split Corp. revealed its plans to initiate a Normal Course Issuer Bid (NCIB) with the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX) approving the company's intention to purchase its Preferred Shares and Class A Shares. The NCIB is set to begin on June 2, 2025, and will run until June 1, 2026. This move signifies the Company's strategy to buy back its own shares utilizing the TSX and/or other Canadian trading systems.
news.headline: Financial 15 Split Corp approved for NCIB to buy Preferred Shares and Class A Shares on TSX for one year starting June 2.

news.summary: Today, Dividend 15 Split Corp. revealed their plan to conduct a Normal Course Issuer Bid (NCIB) to buy back its Preferred Shares and Class A Shares. The Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX) has approved this initiative, allowing the company to repurchase shares through the TSX and/or other Canadian trading platforms. The NCIB is set to begin on June 2, 2025, and run until June 1, 2026, giving the company a year to complete the buyback process.
news.headline: Dividend 15 Split Corp. gets TSX nod for Normal Course Issuer Bid to repurchase shares starting June 2, 2025.

news.summary: Expanding its presence in the Northeast market, a company is increasing its supply of sustainable products made in the USA to meet the growing demand. This includes a variety of products such as Hydro Basil, fresh herbs, and the Pickle Party™ line. The company is aiming to capture a larger share of the market by offering these popular and locally sourced items. The move comes as consumers are increasingly seeking out sustainable and American-made products. This strategic decision is expected to boost the company's sales and solidify its position in the Northeast region.
news.headline: Edible Garden expands distribution to 3 new markets, increasing presence in the Northeast for sustainable, USA products.

news.summary: On May 29, 2025, Context Therapeutics Inc. revealed the appointment of Dr. Karen Chagin, M.D. as the new Chief Medical Officer, effective June 9, 2025. Dr. Chagin will take over from Dr. Karen Smith, M.D., Ph.D., MBA, LLM, who held the position on an interim basis and will continue serving as a member of the Board of Directors. Context is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company traded on Nasdaq under the symbol CNTX, focusing on advancing T cell engaging bispecific antibodies for solid tumors.
news.headline: Context Therapeutics names Dr. Karen Chagin, M.D. as Chief Medical Officer effective June 9, 2025, succeeding Dr. Karen Smith.

news.summary: In the first quarter of 2025, there was a notable increase in the adoption of the company's data collection solutions by prominent AI and eCommerce companies globally. This trend signifies the growing importance and reliance on the company's offerings in the market. The traction gained in Q1 2025 reflects a significant rise in engagement and interest, showcasing the value and effectiveness of the data collection solutions among the key players in the artificial intelligence and eCommerce sectors.
news.headline: Alarum Technologies' Q1 2025 shows increased adoption of data collection solutions among top AI and eCommerce firms.

news.summary: A new product named MycroDose by Nature's Heritage has been introduced by MariMed, featuring a combination of THC and functional mushrooms in a convenient pill format. This innovative offering aims to provide users with a unique blend of benefits from both ingredients. The launch of this product marks MariMed's continued expansion in the market, with consumers now having access to a novel way of incorporating cannabinoids and mushrooms. This development is seen as a response to the growing interest in alternative wellness products.
news.headline: MariMed introduces MycroDose brand, merging THC with functional mushrooms in pill form, expanding into mushroom market.

news.summary: On May 29, 2025, Apogee Therapeutics, Inc. (Nasdaq: APGE), based in San Francisco and Boston, disclosed that key executives are scheduled to engage in a fireside conversation at the Jefferies Global Healthcare Conference on June 5, 2025, at 10:30 a.m. Eastern Time. This event presents an opportunity for subject matter experts to share insights with a broad audience, leveraging the platform to discuss critical aspects of their organization and the healthcare industry at large.
news.headline: Apogee Therapeutics (Nasdaq: APGE) to participate in Jefferies Global Healthcare Conference on June 5, 2025 at 10:30 a.m. ET.

news.summary: A recent report details how the unemployment rate in the United States saw a significant decrease, dropping from 5.8% to 5.2% in just one month. Additionally, the economy added a total of 943,000 new jobs within that same period. This marks a notable improvement in the country's labor market, indicating a positive trajectory towards recovery. The notable reduction in unemployment and substantial job creation are promising signs for the US economy's ongoing rebound from the effects of the global pandemic.
news.headline: Maze Therapeutics is set to join the Jefferies Global Healthcare Conference in 2025.

news.summary: Nidec Corporation, based in Kyoto, Japan, recently disclosed that during a Board of Directors meeting, it was decided to release important information on their website. This information pertains to the challenges faced in conducting audits for their international subsidiaries, which have caused delays in receiving the accounting auditor's report regarding the consolidated financial statements. As a result, stakeholders are encouraged to visit the company's official website for further details on this matter at https://www.nidec.com/en/ir/event/meeting/.
news.headline: Nidec in Japan is delaying financial statements due to audits of overseas units; auditor's report pending.

news.summary: Key points: The SOL/ETH pair has broken below a rising wedge pattern, indicating a potential 40% decline in SOL against ETH. Solana's memecoin revenue has significantly dropped since April, impacting its core value proposition. Standard Chartered warns Solana might underperform as Ethereum's L2 ecosystem grows more competitive. The technical breakdowns and decreasing onchain activity suggest a sharp decline in the SOL/ETH pair. The breakdown aligns with the decline in memecoin-driven activity on Solana, with the largest memecoin launch platform showing reduced daily fee revenue since early April. A report from Standard Chartered highlighted that Solana may struggle if it cannot move beyond memecoins dominating its transaction activity, as Ethereum gains ground with scalable layer-2 solutions. Chartist Alex Clay indicates a potential "Ethereum outperformance season" with confidence in the breakdown on the SOL/ETH charts. This article does not offer investment advice, and readers should conduct their research before making decisions.
news.headline: Solana may face 40% decline vs. Ethereum amid cooling memecoin trend. SOL/ETH breaks wedge pattern, suggesting drop to 0.038 ETH, down 40%.

news.summary: Iguazio and NVIDIA have formed a partnership to enhance AI deployment capabilities by utilizing MLRun and NVIDIA NIM. This collaboration aims to provide scalable and efficient solutions for enterprises working with artificial intelligence. The strategic alliance between the two companies is expected to significantly improve AI deployment processes for businesses.
news.headline: Iguazio and NVIDIA join forces to improve AI deployment with MLRun and NIM, providing advanced solutions for enterprises.

news.summary: In Dubai, United Arab Emirates, the hospitality sector is witnessing a significant increase in shisha popularity, with venue proprietors anticipating a surge in demand in the coming two years. Research from AIR reveals insights into this trend, conducted with 400 hospitality establishments providing shisha in the UAE, Spain, Germany, and the US. The study delves into future demand and trends in these prominent shisha markets globally. The research sheds light on the growing interest in shisha consumption, showcasing the potential for growth in this sector.
news.headline: UAE hospitality venues predict a surge in shisha demand. Study by AIR shows nearly 90% anticipate growth.

news.summary: Silver Storm Mining Ltd. has announced an increase in the size of its best-efforts private placement to meet high investor demand. The offering, led by Red Cloud Securities Inc. and Ventum Financial Corp., will now raise gross proceeds of up to C$6,006,000, up from the initial amount. Silver Storm (TSX.V: SVRS | FSE: SVR) is responding to strong interest from investors, indicating confidence in the company's potential growth and development plans.
news.headline: Silver Storm Mining Ltd. increases private placement size to C$8.0 million due to strong demand. Lead agents Red Cloud Securities Inc. and Ventum Financial Corp. agreed to upsize from C$6,006,000.

news.summary: Coppernico Metals Inc. (TSX: COPR, OTCQB: CPPMF) based in Vancouver, British Columbia, shared plans for its upcoming virtual Annual General Meeting of Shareholders on June 24, 2025, at 10:00 a.m. Pacific Time. Additionally, a leadership fireside chat hosted by 3L Capital is set for June 4, 2025, at 8:00 a.m. Pacific Time.
news.headline: Coppernico hosting virtual AGM on June 24 at 10:00 a.m. PT and fireside chat on June 4 at 8:00 a.m PT with 3L Capital.

news.summary: Toronto, on May 29, 2025 - Flow Capital Corp. (TSXV:FW) has announced the completion of a US$5.0 million senior note investment in Congruity 360, a renowned company in the field of unstructured data management and risk mitigation solutions.
news.headline: Flow Capital Corp. invests $5.0 million in Congruity 360, provider of data management solutions.

news.summary: Build-A-Bear Workshop, Inc. (NYSE: BBW) revealed plans to hold investor meetings at several upcoming conferences. These include NYSE Virtual Investor Access Day on June 5, 2025; DADCO Consumer & Technology Conference on June 10, 2025 in Nashville, Tennessee; Evercore Consumer & Retail Conference on June 11, 2025 in New York City; and Northland Virtual Growth Conference on June 25, 2025. Build-A-Bear is set to engage with investors and industry experts during these events to discuss its business and growth strategies. Founded in [year], Build-A-Bear Workshop focuses on creating unique, interactive retail experiences for customers.
news.headline: Build-A-Bear Workshop (NYSE: BBW) to attend several investor conferences in June 2025; including Tech Conference.

news.summary: Bitcoin has surged to a record high of $111,000 but might face obstacles ahead, as per new data from Glassnode. The analytics company pinpoints $120,000 as a crucial level where historical patterns indicate a surge in selling pressure could occur. Investor profits have sharply increased post the recent rally, with on-chain metrics mirroring past market peaks. Indicators like unrealized profits, spending patterns, and exchange inflows all hint at a rising inclination among investors. More details are accessible on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Glassnode warns of potential Bitcoin sell-off near $120,000 as profit-taking increases. BTC hits new high at $111,000.

news.summary: In recent developments, it has been reported that a record-breaking number of 1,500 new cases of COVID-19 have been confirmed in the past 24 hours. This spike brings the total number of active cases in the region to over 10,000. In response to this surge, the local government has implemented stricter social distancing measures and mandated the closure of all non-essential businesses. Additionally, health officials are urging the public to adhere to safety guidelines and limit interactions to curb the spread of the virus. The healthcare system is currently under immense pressure, with hospitals operating at full capacity and facing shortages of essential supplies. The situation remains critical, and authorities are closely monitoring the situation as they continue efforts to combat the pandemic.
news.headline: Analysis underway on effects of 'Military Keynesianism' on economy. Stay tuned for results. #economics #military

news.summary: Hamilton Lane Incorporated, a private markets asset management firm, announced its fiscal year 2025 results, reporting total assets under management of $138 billion (11% growth YoY) and fee-earning assets under management of $72 billion (10% increase YoY). Revenue from management and advisory fees rose by 14% to $513.9 million. The company's unrealized carried interest balance reached $1.3 billion (up 3% YoY), with GAAP EPS at $5.41 on $217.4 million of GAAP net income for fiscal 2025. A quarterly dividend of $0.54 per share was declared, with a full-year dividend target of $2.16, representing a 10% increase from the previous year. For more details, access the full presentation on the company's Shareholders website. Hamilton Lane will discuss the results in a webcast and conference call on May 29, 2025.
news.headline: Hamilton Lane Inc. saw growth in assets under management by 11% and management/advisory fees by 14% in fiscal year 2025.

news.summary: In Munich, the hospitality industry in Germany is witnessing a significant rise in popularity of shisha, as per recent research from AIR. The study, which surveyed 400 hospitality establishments providing shisha in Germany, Spain, the US, and the UAE, suggests venue owners anticipate a surge in demand over the next two years. The research aims to examine future trends and demand in the top shisha markets globally. The study highlights the growing trend, especially in Germany, with businesses gearing up for increased interest in shisha services.
news.headline: Shisha demand in Germany to see significant growth as 83% of venues anticipate increase; study of 400 businesses projects surging demand in next 2 years.
Crypto Exchange Bybit Granted MiCA License in Austria

news.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is expanding its digital rupee pilots for e₹-Retail and e₹-Wholesale central bank digital currencies (CBDCs), aiming to explore programmability and offline capabilities to increase usability. The e₹-Retail pilot has 600,000 users across 17 banks, while the e₹-Wholesale pilot includes four standalone primary dealers. During FY 2024–25, India saw a 34.8% growth in digital payments volume and a 17.9% increase in value, with the UPI holding a 48.5% share in global real-time payments. India's Supreme Court criticized the lack of regulation on cryptocurrencies despite a 30% tax and expressed concerns about a potential threat to the financial system.
news.headline: RBI expanding digital rupee pilots for retail and wholesale with new use cases; 600,000 users, 34.8% growth in digital payments in India.
INDIA TRADE MINISTRY OFFICIAL: INDIA AND US TRADE TALKS ARE PROGRESSING WELL, EXPECTS GOOD OUTCOME SOON

news.summary: DXL Group reported operating results for Q1 fiscal 2025 with total sales of $105.5 million, down 8.6% from Q1 fiscal 2024. Comparable sales dropped 9.4%, net loss was $(0.04) per share, and adjusted EBITDA was $0.1 million, a decline from $8.2 million in Q1 fiscal 2024. Cash and investments were $29.1 million in May 2025 vs. $53.2 million in May 2024, with no debt. They repurchased $13.6 million in shares. Gross margin decreased to 45.1%, an occupancy cost increase of 280 basis points. SG&A expenses were 45.0% of sales, marketing costs were 6.1%. Net loss for Q1 fiscal 2025 was $1.9 million, $(0.04) per diluted share, compared to net income in Q1 fiscal 2024 of $3.8 million. Cash flow from operations was $(12.0) million, primarily due to earnings decrease. Inventory was $85.5 million. DXL operates 251 retail stores with 1,814,000 sq ft. in May 2025. The article details management comments on initiatives to improve sales, the impact of tariffs, and expectations for Q2 and beyond to enhance performance.
news.headline: Destination XL Group, Inc. reported $105.5 million in total sales, down 8.6%, with a net loss of $(0.04) per diluted share in Q1 2025 compared to $0.06 in Q1 2024. Adjusted EBITDA was $0.1 million compared to $8.2 million in Q1 2024. Total cash and investments were $29.1 million at May 3, 2025, down from $53.2 million at May 4, 2024, with no outstanding debt.

news.summary: SpartanNash, a food solutions company, reported a 3.7% increase in net sales to $2.91 billion for the 16-week first quarter ended April 19, 2025. The Retail segment saw a 19.6% increase in net sales to $947.2 million, with Retail comparable store sales up 1.6%. Adjusted EPS was $0.35 compared to $0.53, and Adjusted EBITDA was $76.9 million compared to $74.9 million. Cash generated from operating activities was $25.8 million, and capital expenditures were $34.6 million. SpartanNash reaffirmed its fiscal 2025 guidance, predicting total net sales between $9.8-10 billion, Adjusted EBITDA between $263-278 million, and Adjusted EPS between $1.60-1.85. The company operates nearly 200 grocery stores and offers support services for independent grocers. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: SpartanNash reports 3.7% sales growth in Q1 fiscal 2025. Net sales reached $2.91 billion; adjusted EBITDA was $76.9 million. Reaffirms fiscal 2025 guidance with total net sales between $9.8-$10.0 billion, adjusted EBITDA between $263-$278 million, and adjusted EPS between $1.60-$1.85. Company operates food wholesale and grocery retail segments, serving various customers. Associated risks and uncertainties highlighted in forward-looking statements. For more details, visit corporate.spartannash.com.

news.summary: LSL Pharma Group Inc., a Canadian pharmaceutical company, reported record quarterly revenues for Q1 of fiscal year 2025, ended March 31, 2025. Revenues were $6.6 million, up 59% from $4.2 million in Q1-24. Operating profit was $0.4 million, up 150%, with a net loss of $0.2 million, down 44%. Adjusted EBITDA was $0.9 million, up 79%. They filed six new Eye-care products with Health Canada and entered strategic agreements for ten new eye-drop products. They faced a court ruling of $0.3 million related to a dispute over plant costs. The corporation plans to contest the ruling. The company appointed Mr. Louis Laflamme to its board of directors and completed the purchase of a new manufacturing line to increase production capacity. The company expressed optimism for future financial performance and development. Total Revenues for Q1-25:CMO: $5,748,000Eye-Care: $877,000Total: $6,625,000
news.headline: LSL Pharma Group reports record revenues in Q1-25: $6.6 million, up 59% from Q1-24. EBITDA up 79%, net loss down 44%. Eye-care revenues down 68%. Expanding product portfolio & facing $0.3M court ruling. Acquisition of new manufacturing line. CEO optimistic for the future.

news.summary: Menomonee Falls based Kohl’s Corporation (NYSE:KSS) announced its first quarter results for the period ended May 3, 2025. There was a 4.1% decline in net sales and a 3.9% decrease in comparable sales. The company saw a 37 basis point increase in gross margin but reported a diluted loss per share of ($0.13). Kohl’s affirmed its full year 2025 financial outlook. Michael Bender, the Interim Chief Executive Officer of Kohl’s, expressed his honor in taking on the role during this critical period for the company, emphasizing the significant potential ahead.
news.headline: Kohl's Q1 fiscal 2025: Net sales down 4.1%, comp sales down 3.9%. Gross margin up 37 bps. Diluted loss/share ($0.13). CEO affirms outlook.

news.summary: Qualified investors can now access non-deliverable crypto derivatives, as confirmed by the Bank of Russia.
news.headline: Qualified investors can now access non-deliverable crypto derivatives, as per Bank of Russia.

news.summary: During the initial trial, the first participant experienced a substantial reduction in seizure frequency by 92% without any significant adverse events. This positive outcome was observed over the course of five months of follow-up.
news.headline: First patient in uniQure's trial for AMT-260 in MTLE shows 92% reduction in seizures in 5 months with no serious adverse events.

news.summary: On May 29, 2025, in Waltham, Massachusetts, Zenas BioPharma, Inc. (Nasdaq: ZBIO), a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on treatments for autoimmune diseases, revealed plans for a presentation at the Jefferies Global Healthcare Conference on June 5, 2025, at 1:25 p.m. ET. Zenas aims to be a key player in developing and marketing therapies for autoimmune diseases.
news.headline: Zenas BioPharma to present at Jefferies Global Healthcare Conference on June 5, 2025, at 1:25 p.m. ET.

news.summary: In Plantation, Fla., and Bentonville, Ark., a partnership between NationsBenefits® and Walmart has been established to revolutionize access to crucial benefits for health plan members through technological solutions. NationsBenefits® is a prominent healthcare fintech, supplemental benefits, and outcomes platform, while Walmart is recognized as the biggest retailer globally. The collaboration aims to enhance the way individuals access necessary benefits, utilizing NationsBenefits’ Basket Analyzer Service (BAS) and Walmart Everyday Health Signals™ program. This joint effort signifies a significant step towards providing improved services to plan members and is a testament to the commitment of both entities to innovate the healthcare industry.
news.headline: NationsBenefits and Walmart team up to enhance health plan engagement through tech integration, focusing on Basket Analyzer Service.

news.summary: A technology company based in San Francisco, DreamPark, has revealed that it has successfully raised $1.1 million in seed funding. This financial boost was spearheaded by Long Journey Ventures, with additional contributions from Founders Inc. DreamPark is renowned for being the creator of the world's largest downloadable mixed reality theme park. Their primary goal is to revamp mundane surroundings into thrilling adventures using cutting-edge mixed reality (XR) technology. By obtaining this investment, DreamPark aims to accelerate its efforts in making Earth an exciting playground once again. The company stands out as a leader in mixed reality innovation, continuously pushing boundaries in this field.
news.headline: DreamPark gets $1.1M seed funding to convert real spaces to mixed reality parks, led by Long Journey Ventures and Founders Inc.

news.summary: Minneapolis-based retail giant Best Buy Co., Inc. (NYSE: BBY) released its financial results for the first quarter ending on May 3, 2025 (“Q1 FY26”), in comparison to the same period in fiscal year 2024 (“Q1 FY25”). Total revenue in Q1 FY26 amounted to $8.767 billion for the Enterprise, slightly lower than the $8.847 billion reported in Q1 FY25. In terms of segments, the Domestic sector saw revenues of $8.127 billion (compared to $8.203 billion in Q1 FY25) while the International segment brought in $640 million (down from $644 million in Q1 FY25). The Enterprise comparable sales witnessed a 0.7% decrease in Q1 FY26, compared to a 6.1% decline in Q1 FY25. Similarly, Domestic comparable sales dipped by 0.7% in Q1 FY26, in contrast to a 6.3% drop noted in Q1 FY25.
news.headline: Best Buy's Q1 FY26 revenue: $8.767B, down from $8.847B in Q1 FY25 with comparable sales % change at -0.7% domestically.

news.summary: DCG, a worldwide investor, creator, and nurturer of decentralized ecosystems, has disclosed findings from a recent study in collaboration with The Harris Poll. The research indicates that 75% of consumers have faith in decentralized AI to bolster innovation and advancement over centralized AI. The survey reached 2,036 U.S. participants, including registered voters and AI users, showcasing a rising preference for decentralized artificial intelligence strategies.
news.headline: 75% of Americans prefer decentralized AI innovation over centralized AI, as revealed in DCG and The Harris Poll study.
